payload.project_name: 116th Street Bus and Pedestrian Priority Project
payload.description: Project proposes curbside bus lane on Manhattan Avenue between 110th St and 116th St, pedestrian space repurposing slip lane on Morningside Avenue, concrete pedestrian islands, bus stop removal/consolidation, and painted curb extension at Morningside Avenue and 116th Street.

tool_args.label: Bus Stop Removal - Manhattan Avenue / W. 112th Street
tool_args.raw_text: Proposed Bus Stop Removal on Manhattan Av. (1 stop) Stop consolidation will speed up bus by removing closely spaced/underutilized stop at Manhattan Av./W. 112 th St.

payload.description: Remove stop #400484 at Manhattan Av./112th St. Below minimum spacing of 750 feet. Provides ~5 new overnight/weekend parking spots.

tool_args.label: Painted Curb Extension - Morningside Avenue and 116th Street
tool_args.raw_text: Proposed Painted Curb Extension (1 intersection) Daylights space between crosswalks for pedestrian safety with hardened elements

payload.description: Painted curb extension at intersection of Morningside Avenue and 116th Street. Includes bike corral for 18+ bicycles. Helps reduce crossing distance by 39% and 34%. Daylights space between crosswalks with hardened safety treatments like granite blocks and vertical delineators.

payload.description: Repurpose approximately 3 parking spaces to daylight intersection with hardened safety treatments like granite blocks and vertical delineators. Uses proposed pedestrian curb extension for bike corral for 18+ bicycles.

tool_args.label: +22s green time per 90s cycle from two-phase signal

payload.description: Simpler two-phase signal saves 22 seconds of green time per 90s light cycle for Manhattan Avenue NB/SB traffic vs three-phase signal.
payload.comparison: 3-phase: 45s green for Manhattan Ave NB/SB + 23s for 113th St + 22s for Morningside Ave SB/Manhattan Ave NB = 90s. 2-phase: 67s for Manhattan Ave NB/SB + 23s for 113th St = 90s
